name: rust
description: Idiomatic Rust conventions, error handling, testing, async, and tooling for production code

decisions.decision: Always use the latest stable Rust edition; set rust-version (MSRV) in Cargo.toml
decisions.rationale: Each edition unlocks safety improvements and language features. The latest edition (currently 2024) adds critical safety defaults like unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn warnings. MSRV communicates the minimum compiler version to downstream users.
decisions.details: Set edition in workspace Cargo.toml. Migrate with cargo fix --edition. Set rust-version to minimum supported compiler. CI: test on stable + MSRV.

decisions.decision: Use thiserror for library error types, anyhow for application error propagation; never use unwrap() in production code paths
decisions.rationale: thiserror provides zero-cost derive macros for std::error::Error without appearing in your public API. anyhow provides easy context addition with .context(). unwrap() panics on failure — use ? to propagate or expect() with a reason when panic is justified.
decisions.details: Libs: #[derive(thiserror::Error)] enums, #[from] for conversion, #[source] for chains. Apps: anyhow::Result + .context(). Alt: snafu (rich selectors), miette (CLI diagnostics). Enable clippy::unwrap_used in prod, allow in tests. Panic only via expect("reason").

decisions.decision: Configure lints in Cargo.toml with workspace inheritance; enable pedantic and nursery with selective allows
decisions.rationale: Cargo.toml lint configuration (RFC 3389) is the modern approach — workspace inheritance ensures consistency across all crates. Pedantic catches real issues but has noisy lints that should be selectively allowed. Restriction group should never be enabled wholesale — cherry-pick specific lints.
decisions.details: [workspace.lints.clippy]: all/pedantic/nursery/cargo at warn, priority -1. Allow: cast_precision_loss, module_name_repetitions, must_use_candidate. Restriction cherry-picks: dbg_macro, undocumented_unsafe_blocks, unwrap_used. Members: [lints] workspace = true. CI: clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features, RUSTFLAGS="-Dwarnings".

decisions.decision: Use Cargo workspaces with crates/ directory; separate lib.rs from main.rs even in binaries
decisions.rationale: Workspaces provide dependency and lint inheritance. Separating lib.rs from main.rs enables unit testing without running the binary and allows library reuse.
decisions.details: Layout: Cargo.toml (workspace root), crates/core/ (types/traits), crates/cli/, crates/server/. Simple projects: lib.rs + thin main.rs (parse args, wire deps, call lib). Use workspace = true for deps, metadata, lints. Set publish = false for internal crates. Commit Cargo.lock for binaries only.

decisions.decision: Multi-layered testing with cargo-nextest as runner; property testing with proptest; snapshot testing with insta
decisions.rationale: cargo-nextest is up to 3x faster than cargo test with per-test process isolation and flaky test detection. proptest catches edge cases manual tests miss. insta makes golden-file testing trivial with cargo insta review.
decisions.details: Layers: unit (#[cfg(test)]), property (proptest), snapshot (insta + cargo insta review), integration (tests/), doc tests. Use rstest for parameterized/fixtures, mockall for trait mocking. Target 80%+ coverage via cargo-llvm-cov. CI: cargo nextest run --workspace.

decisions.decision: Use tokio as the async runtime; keep domain logic synchronous; async only at I/O boundaries
decisions.rationale: Tokio is the dominant async runtime (20,000+ crates depend on it). async-std is discontinued (March 2025). Async adds complexity — only use it when you need high-concurrency I/O. Keeping domain logic synchronous makes it testable and composable.
decisions.details: #[tokio::main] for apps. tokio::spawn_blocking for CPU work. .instrument(span) for async tracing context. Prefer multi-threaded runtime; current_thread for constrained envs. Anti-patterns: blocking in async (use spawn_blocking), unnecessary async, missing Send + 'static on spawned tasks.

decisions.decision: Use tracing for all logging and observability; never use println! or the log crate directly
decisions.rationale: tracing is the de facto standard for Rust logging, developed by the Tokio team. It supports structured logging, spans, async context propagation, and OpenTelemetry integration. It supersedes the log crate.
decisions.details: tracing-subscriber for config (fmt layer, JSON, EnvFilter for RUST_LOG). tracing-log bridge for log-crate deps. Structured fields: info!(user_id = %id, "msg") -- no string interpolation. .instrument(span) on futures. tracing-opentelemetry for distributed tracing.

decisions.decision: Avoid writing unsafe code; when necessary, minimize scope, document safety invariants, and test with Miri
decisions.rationale: Most unsafe functionality exists in the standard library or established crates. Unsafe blocks that lack safety documentation are time bombs. Miri detects undefined behavior that tests and sanitizers miss.
decisions.details: Prefer Rc/Arc/RefCell/Mutex/Pin over raw pointers. One op per unsafe block. Every block needs // SAFETY: comment. Wrap unsafe in safe-interface modules. Run cargo +nightly miri test. Enable clippy::undocumented_unsafe_blocks. Edition 2024 warns on unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn by default.

decisions.decision: All public API items must have doc comments with examples; use deny(missing_docs) in library crates
decisions.rationale: Rust doc comments are runnable tests — they serve as both documentation and regression tests. The Rust API Guidelines require documentation with examples for all public items.
decisions.details: Structure: summary, explanation, # Examples (runnable), # Errors, # Panics, # Safety. Use /// for items, //! for modules. Examples use ? not unwrap(). Review with cargo doc --no-deps --open. Set #![deny(missing_docs)] in lib crate roots. Run cargo test --doc.

decisions.decision: Use workspace dependency inheritance; run cargo-deny for license compliance and vulnerability scanning
decisions.rationale: Workspace inheritance eliminates version duplication across crates. cargo-deny subsumes cargo-audit and adds license, ban, and source checks — critical for production software.
decisions.details: Define deps once in [workspace.dependencies]; members use dep.workspace = true. SemVer ranges ("1.2" not "=1.2.3"). Commit Cargo.lock for binaries. Configure deny.toml for advisories/licenses/bans/sources; run in CI per PR. Weekly cargo audit as backup. Use cargo-semver-checks before publishing libs.

decisions.decision: Use rustfmt with default settings; enforce in CI with cargo fmt --check
decisions.rationale: rustfmt eliminates all formatting debates. Default settings match community convention. Consistent formatting reduces review noise.
decisions.details: CI: cargo fmt --all -- --check. Minimal rustfmt.toml customization. Use #[rustfmt::skip] for exceptions.

decisions.decision: Profile before optimizing; use criterion or divan for benchmarks; track regressions in CI
decisions.rationale: Micro-benchmarks can be misleading — always profile realistic workloads first. criterion provides statistical analysis with historical tracking. divan is the modern alternative with simpler API and less timing noise in CI.
decisions.details: criterion: HTML reports. divan: CI-friendly (less noise). iai-callgrind: deterministic instruction counts. Use black_box in benchmarks. Profile with flamegraph/cargo-instruments first. Perf crates: rayon, dashmap, smallvec, parking_lot.
